description: v7.1 amendment to §5.1 OR-fusion. Maps the Layer-1 detection outcome (which safety signals fired + language) to a crisis TIER, so the RESPONSE is graded while DETECTION stays sensitive. safety_check reads the first matching rule's tier and hardcodes no boundary. Clinician-editable. No phrase/keyword lists (phrase tuning stays in the S3 FP calibration set). v1.1.0 adds the conservative language gate + fail-closed catch-all (the T1 warm route is safe ONLY on confident English; language ID is weakest for Arabizi/code-switch). Signed 2026-07-03 (G1) + language-gate closure.

signal_semantics.s1_fired: any Layer-1 KEYWORD flag fired (e.g. si_explicit, si_passive, crisis keyword) — i.e. any fired flag other than s3_semantic
signal_semantics.lang_in: detected_language is one of the listed codes
signal_semantics.require_confident_lang: resolver-computed: lang=='en' AND not code_switching AND not arabizi_suspect. Guards the T1 route against an Arabizi/code-switched true-SI message misread as English.

rules.note: Any keyword corroboration -> acute crisis (RED), all languages. The T2 safety floor.

CONFIDENT English semantic-only signal -> warm concern (offer-not-force). Fixes the prod false-RED on 'i am feeling hopeless' / 'burden ... so low'. Only fires when language is confidently English (not code-switched, not Arabizi-suspect).
Arabic/Arabizi semantic signal -> acute; S1 keyword coverage is weak there, so S3 remains load-bearing (preserves the Arabic command-hallucination catch).

FAIL-CLOSED catch-all: any semantic signal not confidently-English (low-confidence EN, code-switch, Arabizi, or a language no rule maps) routes acute. Never let a fired crisis signal fall through to 'none'.
